assertions.text: V1 was entering the roadway from a parking spot.
The roadway is five lanes and two-way.
V1 was attempting to make a U-turn to head west.
V2 was traveling east in lane two of the five-lane road.
As V1 made the U-turn, V1's left side made contact with V2's front plane.
As the vehicles continued in motion after initial contact, V1's left side struck the front of V2.
There was at least one impact between V1 and V2 (initial left-side to front-plane contact), followed by an additional contact where V1's left side struck V2's front (total of at least two contacts).
The impact involved V1's left side contacting V2's front (V2 was struck in the front by V1's left side).
V1 came to final rest in the westbound lanes of travel.
V2 came to final rest in the middle of the roadway.

verdicts.evidence: The claim that V1 was entering from a parking spot cannot be decided from V2 telemetry alone; no channel reports other vehicle positions or parking-spot status in the available data.
Road geometry (five lanes, two-way) is not present in the vehicle telemetry; sensors do not record number of lanes or road direction.
V2 telemetry cannot determine V1's maneuver or intended heading; there is no heading or map-level intent for V1 in the provided channels.
V2 speed and steering are present but lane assignment and compass heading (east) are not available in the telemetry; the data do not state V2 was in lane two headed east.

Device-frame crash axes are undocumented and the telemetry cannot attribute which side of the other vehicle contacted which part of V2; delta-V shows a dominant longitudinal change of [ -18.0 km/h ](T2) and lateral of [ -5.0 km/h ](T2) but that does not uniquely identify 'V1 left side to V2 front plane.'

The assertion that after initial contact V1's left side struck V2's front is a multi-step positional claim about V1; V2 telemetry shows a crash pulse with cumulative longitudinal delta-V [ -18.0 km/h ](T2) and lateral delta-V peak [ -5.0 km/h ](T2) but cannot confirm multiple contacts or which side of the other vehicle struck V2.

The EDR summary reports [num_events_recorded: 1](T1) and the crash-pulse data show a single continuous delta-V curve with final longitudinal delta-V [ -18.0 km/h ](T2), which contradicts the claim of at least two separate impacts between the vehicles.

Telemetry shows a primarily longitudinal delta-V of [ -18.0 km/h ](T2) and a smaller lateral delta-V peak of [ -5.0 km/h ](T2), but device-frame axes to vehicle-side mapping is undocumented, so attributing the impact to 'V1's left side contacting V2's front' is not decidable from V2 telemetry alone.
Final rest position of V1 (in westbound lanes) is about scene geometry and not recorded in V2 telemetry; no GPS trajectory for other vehicles is available.

V2 final speed at t=0 (impact) is [35.0 km/h](T2) and the EDR shows a cumulative longitudinal delta-V final value of [ -18.0 km/h ](T2) during the crash pulse, consistent with a significant in-roadway collision and the EDR vehicle context notes 'vehicles_in_crash: 2' and final rest in roadway is consistent with coming to rest in the middle of the roadway, but exact position is not directly recorded. The telemetry alone cannot precisely locate lateral final rest, so this is marginal; however the event summary's 'vehicles_in_crash: 2' and crash pulse magnitudes support V2 stopping in roadway.

result.rationale: V2 telemetry provides strong evidence of a single significant crash pulse dominated by longitudinal delta-V [ -18.0 km/h ](T2) and smaller lateral delta-V [ -5.0 km/h ](T2) and pre-crash speed rising to [35.0 km/h](T2). Positional and third-vehicle maneuver details (lane, parking spot, which side of V1 struck V2, multiple discrete impacts) are not available from these channels, making many narrative assertions unverifiable.

result.time_zero: impact (t=0); pre-crash negative, crash pulse positive

channels.name: delta_v_long
channels.desc: Cumulative longitudinal delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

channels.name: delta_v_lat
channels.desc: Cumulative lateral delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

result.notes: NHTSA CISS EDR data: sparse pre-crash series (~2 Hz, t<0) plus a crash-pulse delta-V curve (t>=0, ~10 ms steps). t=0 is the impact.

delta_v_long.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

delta_v_lat.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse
